title: ctenopteris confusa
intro: Ctenopteris confusa is a species of fern native to New Zealand, characterized by its delicate fronds and ability to thrive in shaded, humid environments. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Ctenopteris confusa include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as providing relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and topical applications, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
